title: Sorting Algorithms, Stability, and Ordering Contracts · Correctness Defense
summary: Order customer records by multiple keys while preserving meaningful prior order. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a sorting laboratory with operation counts. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define total, partial, and key-based orderings including tie and missing-value policy.
Trace insertion and selection sorting invariants and identify useful input regimes.
Implement stable merge sort and account for auxiliary space and copying.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
